commands.review_summary:
The branch changes `applyJobResult` so unresolved transient-error retries for `every` cron jobs clear `nextRunAtMs` with a warning, and adds a focused regression test.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +59. Total +69 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Source-level yes, real-runtime no: current `main` falls back to `backoffNext` when an `every` retry cannot compute `normalNext`, but I did not establish a live scheduler repro.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is contributor real-behavior proof plus maintainer acceptance of the fallback behavior, not an automation-friendly code repair.

target.title: fix(cron):cron-every-error-backoff-unresolved
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`every` cron jobs could fall back to a pure backoff schedule during transient
error retry when next-run resolution returned `undefined`.

In `src/cron/service/timer.ts`, the error-backoff path already guards
unresolved `cron` next runs through `resolveCronNextRunWithLowerBound()`, which
logs a warning and clears the schedule. The corresponding `every` path instead
used `backoffNext` when `normalNext` was `undefined`, which silently changed the
job's retry cadence away from the configured recurring schedule.

This patch makes unresolved transient retries for `every` jobs warn and clear
`nextRunAtMs` instead of falling back to a pure backoff timestamp, and adds a
focused regression test for that path.

## Evidence

Commands run:

```text
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.issue-66019-unresolved-next-run.test.ts
```

Observed output:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  4 passed (4)
```

Behavior addressed: transient error retries for `every` cron jobs now emit a
warning and clear the schedule when next-run resolution returns `undefined`,
instead of silently rescheduling from a pure backoff timestamp.

Real environment tested: Local checkout on branch
`codex/cron-every-error-backoff-unresolved` from latest `upstream/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.issue-66019-unresolved-next-run.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
src/cron/service.issue-66019-unresolved-next-run.test.ts >
#66019 unresolved next-run repro >
does not refire a recurring cron job 2s later when next-run resolution returns undefined
src/cron/service.issue-66019-unresolved-next-run.test.ts >
#66019 unresolved next-run repro >
does not refire a recurring errored cron job after the first backoff window when next-run resolution returns undefined
src/cron/service.issue-66019-unresolved-next-run.test.ts >
#66019 unresolved next-run repro >
warns and clears schedule when an every-type retry cannot compute its next run
src/cron/service.issue-66019-unresolved-next-run.test.ts >
#66019 unresolved next-run repro >
preserves the active error backoff floor when maintenance repair later finds a natural next run

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  4 passed (4)
```

Observed result after fix: when an `every` job hits a transient error retry and
its normal next run cannot be computed, OpenClaw now logs a warning with the
job id, job name, and schedule kind, then clears `nextRunAtMs` instead of
switching to a pure backoff-only schedule. The existing unresolved-next-run
regression behavior remains green.

What was not tested: broader cron suites beyond the focused unresolved-next-run
regression file.
target.author: hugenshen
